rows.label: negative
rows.right: He/she/it **is not (isn't / 's not) playing** ... You/we/they **are not (aren't / 're not) playing** ...

content.subtitle: Use
items: Fixed plans and arrangements: I**'m meeting** Clara for lunch on Friday.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Future plans that have not yet been arranged are called 'intentions'. We do not use the present continuous for intentions. We use *be going to* instead. ✗ I**'m becoming** an explorer when I grow up.
We do not use the present continuous for predictions. We use *will* or *be going to* instead. ✗ Do you think you**'re enjoying** your trip to Berlin next week?

content.subtitle: Use
items: Certain future facts: The new bridge **will** be two kilometres long.
Predictions based on opinion or belief: You**'ll** love the food in Mexico.
Offers and requests: I**'ll** carry those bags for you.
Spontaneous decisions made at the moment of speaking: It's cold in here — I**'ll** close the window.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: For offers in question form with *I* and *we*, use *Shall*. ✓ **Shall** I drive you to the airport?
We do not use *will* for arrangements. ✗ We**'ll** visit my grandma this weekend.

rows.label: statement
rows.right: He/she/it **is ('s) going to** travel ... You/we/they **are ('re) going to** travel ...

rows.label: negative
rows.right: He/she/it **is not (isn't / 's not) going to** travel ... You/we/they **are not (aren't / 're not) going to** travel ...

rows.label: question
rows.right: **Is** he/she/it **going to** travel ...? **Are** you/we/they **going to** travel ...?

content.subtitle: Use
items: Plans and intentions for the future: I**'m going to** study medicine when I finish school.
Predictions based on something we can observe now: Watch out — that vase **is going to** fall!
Certain future facts: The new airport **is going to** be the biggest in Europe.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Scheduled events (timetables): The ferry **departs** at seven in the evening.

sections.title: D: Original passage gap fill practice
sections.instructions: Complete the original passage using the best answers for the gaps.
sections.passage:
Original passage for Future time

Tom joined a online lesson because the group wanted fresh practice for passage gap fill practice. The tutor wrote (1) ...... (2) ...... on the board and asked everyone to explain their choices. Later, the group compared (3) ...... (4) ...... with the rule from the lesson. The text is new, but it practises the same passage gap fill.
